evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensuring compliance with architecture, security and access standards together with IT, Architecture and Security teams
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities repeatedly emphasise data quality, reliability, governance, and compliance with architecture/security standards, with no velocity/speed language present, making this rigour-dominated.
evidence.domain_risk: Ensuring compliance with architecture, security and access standards
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role affects internal decision-making across a global agricultural company with compliance framing limited to architecture/security standards rather than financial or regulatory reporting, keeping it moderate.
evidence.collaboration_width: product owners; analytics engineers; IT specialists; business stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A global data platform, dedicated Architecture and Security teams, and multiple named specialised roles (product owners, analytics engineers, IT specialists) indicate an established, mature organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Designing, developing and maintaining advanced data models and analytics products using tools like Databricks, dbt and Power BI
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tools (Databricks, dbt, Power BI, DAX, Azure DP203) and concrete practices (lifecycle management, self-service BI) give a clear picture of daily work, consistent with hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Working closely with product owners, analytics engineers, IT specialists and business stakeholders
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Named partners span both the internal data/IT function and general business stakeholders with no single one dominating, giving mixed orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Leading complex analytics and reporting initiatives with a mid- to long-term horizon
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role leads initiatives with a strategic time horizon and coaches colleagues, a clear strategic ownership framing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate to use or AI systems the candidate's output feeds.
evidence.testing_framing: Experience with DevOps practices such as version control, testing and CI/CD for data products
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing appears within a DevOps practices requirement rather than as its own owned responsibility with a dedicated ownership verb, closer to tool_listed.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensuring compliance with architecture, security and access standards together with IT, Architecture and Security teams
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Compliance and governance are named concerns tied to platform reliability and security rather than explicit regulatory/audit risk, placing it at moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Improving data quality, reliability, performance and cost efficiency through lifecycle management
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant framing is improving and governing an existing global data platform rather than building new systems from scratch, a fix_scale framing.
evidence.loss_aversion: Ensuring compliance with architecture, security and access standards — compliance framing is scoped to platform governance, not regulatory/financial risk.
